title: Rewrite an async-false module to async-true using allow/3
prompt: A module `test/my_app/notification_service_test.exs` is `async: false` because its setup starts a GenServer under a supervisor that it passes connections to via shared mode. Rewrite to `test/my_app/notification_service_test.exs`: (1) `async: true`, (2) use `start_supervised!` to tie GenServer lifetime to the test, (3) use `allow/3` to grant the GenServer sandbox access, (4) do NOT use `mode(:shared)` at all. Include at least one test that verifies DB writes by the GenServer.
tier_rationale: Three-step refactor: start_supervised + allow + remove shared mode. Requires full workflow.
research_citation: "Because allowances use an explicit mechanism, their advantage is that you can still run your tests in async mode." — Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox docs
